_meta.notes: Rhozell, the Wyrm's hand. Phase 5.5 Day 34-35 named antagonist. The Long Wyrm is a force; Rhozell is a character — vindictive, formal, eloquent. He cites the Wyrm in every third sentence and collects debts. Players who killed him in a past life see him return with a remembered grudge ('you remember the slime that ended me; this time will be different'). Rhozell can 'die' multiple times — he's an avatar of the Wyrm's intent, not a single body. Each death just resets him with one more mark in his ledger.

tagline: tall, narrow, dressed in ash-grey scale that fits like skin. when he speaks, he speaks slowly.

behavior.default: stands too still, watches with too few blinks; speaks in carefully arranged sentences.
behavior.ifAttacked: responds with measured violence; does not panic; does not raise his voice.
narrativeHooks: Rhozell never says 'the Wyrm.' He says 'my master.' Or sometimes, just 'it.'
He is patient in the way only servants of long-lived things are.
When he leaves a room, the temperature falls noticeably.

interactions.speak: available; replies are short, formal, often citing 'my master.'
interactions.trade: rejected — 'I do not bargain. I collect.'
interactions.absorb: rejected — 'you would not survive what is in me.'

trashTalk: my master will not be amused.
you have wagered more than you brought to the table.
this will be remembered. as all things are.
dueling.refusalLine: I do not duel softlings. there is paperwork.

metadata.voice: vindictive, formal, eloquent — cites his master in every third sentence
